message:
feat(router): route priority scoring and enhanced match semantics

Extend trie router with priority-based route matching:
- Add route priority calculation based on segment specificity
- Literal segments score higher than typed parameters
- Typed parameters ({id:int}) score higher than wildcards
- Implement consistent tie-breaking for overlapping routes

Improve middleware CSRF implementation:
- Refactor token validation logic
- Add dependency on hmac crate for signed tokens

Update match.rs with improved parameter extraction
Update lib.rs exports for new functionality

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-27ju","title":"Add rich_rust dependency and feature flag integration","description":"# Add rich_rust Dependency Integration

## Task Overview

Configure the rich_rust dependency properly in fastapi-output, including feature flag
handling so that rich output can be optionally compiled out for minimal builds.

## Why Feature Flags Matter

1. **Binary Size**: rich_rust adds ~2MB+ to binary; agents may not need it
2. **Compile Time**: Faster builds when rich features disabled
3. **Dependency Control**: Some environments may not want rich_rust's transitive deps
4. **CI Optimization**: Can test plain mode without rich compilation

## Cargo.toml Configuration

```toml
[package]
name = "fastapi-output"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"

[dependencies]
# Terminal detection (always needed, small)
crossterm = "0.28"

# For global instance
once_cell = "1.19"

# Rich output library - optional
rich_rust = { path = "/dp/rich_rust", optional = true }

[features]
default = ["rich"]
rich = ["dep:rich_rust"]
# Full features includes syntax highlighting etc
full = ["rich", "rich_rust/full"]
```

## Conditional Compilation Pattern

Throughout the crate, use this pattern:

```rust
#[cfg(feature = "rich")]
use rich_rust::prelude::*;

impl RichOutput {
    #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
    fn render_table_rich(&self, headers: &[&str], rows: &[Vec<String>]) {
        // Use rich_rust Table
    }
    
    #[cfg(not(feature = "rich"))]
    fn render_table_rich(&self, headers: &[&str], rows: &[Vec<String>]) {
        // Fallback to plain
        self.render_table_plain(headers, rows);
    }
}
```

## Feature Detection at Runtime

```rust
/// Check if rich output is available at compile time.
pub const fn has_rich_support() -> bool {
    cfg!(feature = "rich")
}

/// Get description of available features.
pub fn feature_info() -> &'static str {
    if cfg!(feature = "full") {
        "full (rich output with syntax highlighting)"
    } else if cfg!(feature = "rich") {
        "rich (styled output with tables and panels)"
    } else {
        "plain (text only, no dependencies)"
    }
}
```

## Usage in fastapi Crate

The main fastapi crate should re-export with same feature flags:

```toml
# crates/fastapi/Cargo.toml
[dependencies]
fastapi-output = { path = "../fastapi-output", optional = true }

[features]
default = ["output"]
output = ["dep:fastapi-output", "fastapi-output/rich"]
output-plain = ["dep:fastapi-output"]
full = ["output", "fastapi-output/full"]
```

## Build Verification

```bash
# Build with rich (default)
cargo build -p fastapi-output

# Build without rich (plain only)
cargo build -p fastapi-output --no-default-features

# Build with full features
cargo build -p fastapi-output --features full

# Verify size difference
ls -la target/release/libfastapi_output.rlib  # With rich
ls -la target/release/libfastapi_output.rlib  # Without rich
```

## Graceful Degradation

When rich feature is disabled:
- OutputMode::Rich becomes OutputMode::Plain automatically
- All rich methods have plain fallbacks
- No rich_rust types in public API
- Compile-time enforcement (no runtime panics)

## Test Matrix

| Feature Flags | Rich Available | Default Mode |
|---------------|----------------|--------------|
| default (rich) | Yes | Auto-detect |
| none | No | Plain |
| full | Yes + syntax | Auto-detect |

## Acceptance Criteria

1. cargo build succeeds with default features
2. cargo build succeeds with --no-default-features
3. cargo build succeeds with --features full
4. Rich mode unavailable when feature disabled
5. No compile errors in either configuration
6. Feature detection functions work correctly
7.  {"id":"bd-29lr","title":"Phase 1: Foundation - fastapi-output Crate and Core Infrastructure","description":"# Phase 1: Foundation

## Purpose

Establish the core infrastructure for rich output integration by creating the new fastapi-output 
crate with agent detection, mode switching, and the RichOutput facade API.

## Why This Phase First

Everything else depends on this foundation:
- Agent detection determines which mode to use
- OutputMode enum is used by all components
- RichOutput facade is the unified API all other phases call
- Theme system defines colors used everywhere

Without this phase, no other rich output work can proceed.

## Deliverables

1. **New crate: fastapi-output** (crates/fastapi-output/)
   - Cargo.toml with rich_rust as optional dependency
   - Module structure for all subcomponents
   - Re-exports for convenient API access

2. **Agent Detection System** (detection.rs)
   - is_agent_environment() function
   - Environment variable checks (CLAUDE_CODE, CODEX_CLI, etc.)
   - TTY detection via crossterm
   - NO_COLOR standard support
   - FORCE_COLOR override

3. **OutputMode System** (mode.rs)
   - OutputMode enum (Rich, Plain, Minimal)
   - Mode selection logic
   - Runtime mode switching support

4. **RichOutput Facade** (facade.rs)
   - Central RichOutput struct
   - Mode-aware method dispatching
   - Global instance management (lazy static)
   - Builder pattern for configuration

5. **Theme System** (themes.rs)
   - FastApiTheme struct with all color definitions
   - ThemePreset enum for built-in themes
   - Color constants for HTTP methods, status codes
   - Theme loading and application

## Technical Decisions

### Why a Separate Crate?

1. **Modularity**: Can be disabled entirely via feature flag
2. **Compile Time**: Only compiled when needed
3. **Dependency Isolation**: rich_rust dependency contained
4. **Testing**: Can test output logic independently

### Why Optional rich_rust Dependency?

When the "rich" feature is disabled:
- No rich_rust code compiled
- Plain mode is the only mode
- Minimal binary size impact
- Zero runtime overhead

### Global Instance vs Passed Reference?

Using a global lazy_static instance because:
- Output happens everywhere in the codebase
- Passing RichOutput through every function is impractical
- One-time initialization is fine for this use case
- Thread-safe via LazyLock

## Files to Create

```
crates/fastapi-output/
├── Cargo.toml
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs           # Public API, prelude
│   ├── detection.rs     # Agent environment detection
│   ├── mode.rs          # OutputMode enum and logic
│   ├── facade.rs        # RichOutput struct
│   └── themes.rs        # Theme definitions
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `cargo build -p fastapi-output` succeeds
2. `cargo build -p fastapi-output --no-default-features` succeeds (plain mode only)
3. Agent detection correctly identifies Claude Code, Codex, etc.
4. Mode switching works at runtime
5. Theme colors render correctly in rich mode
6.  {"id":"bd-2buz","title":"Implement OpenAPI endpoint table display","description":"## Task: OpenAPI Endpoint Table Display

### Parent Feature
Phase 5: OpenAPI Display & Visual Polish (bd-urty)

### Overview
Create a visual table display of all OpenAPI-documented endpoints, showing methods, paths, summaries, and tags in a scannable format.

### Background & Reasoning
When developing APIs, having a quick overview of all endpoints helps:
- Verify all routes are documented
- Check for naming consistency
- Understand API scope at a glance
- Debug missing endpoints

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structures
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/openapi_display.rs

pub struct OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
    pub endpoints: Vec<EndpointInfo>,
    pub group_by_tag: bool,
    pub show_responses: bool,
}

pub struct EndpointInfo {
    pub method: HttpMethod,
    pub path: String,
    pub operation_id: Option<String>,
    pub summary: Option<String>,
    pub tags: Vec<String>,
    pub deprecated: bool,
    pub auth_required: bool,
    pub response_codes: Vec<u16>,
}
```

#### Visualization

**Rich Mode**:
```
┌──────────────────────── API Endpoints ────────────────────────┐
│                                                                │
│ 12 endpoints documented                                        │
│                                                                │
│ Users                                                          │
│ ┌────────┬─────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐│
│ │ GET    │ /users              │ List all users               ││
│ │ POST   │ /users              │ Create a new user     🔒     ││
│ │ GET    │ /users/{id}         │ Get user by ID               ││
│ │ PUT    │ /users/{id}         │ Update user           🔒     ││
│ │ DELETE │ /users/{id}         │ Delete user           🔒     ││

│ │ GET    │ /items              │ List all items               ││
│ │ POST   │ /items              │ Create an item        🔒     ││

### Implementation
```rust
impl OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        let mut content = Vec::new();
        
        content.push(String::new());
        content.push(format!("  {} endpoints documented", self.endpoints.len()));
        content.push(String::new());
        
        // Group by tag if enabled
        let groups = if self.group_by_tag {
            self.group_endpoints_by_tag()
        } else {
            vec![("All".to_string(), self.endpoints.clone())]
        };
        
        for (tag, endpoints) in groups {
            if self.group_by_tag {
                content.push(theme.style_text(&format!("  {}", tag), "bold"));
            }
            
            let mut table = Table::new()
                .with_column(Column::new("").width(8))
                .with_column(Column::new("").width(25))
                .with_column(Column::new("").max_width(40));
            
            for ep in &endpoints {
                let method_styled = theme.style_method(&ep.method);
                
                let mut summary = ep.summary.clone().unwrap_or_default();
                
                // Add indicators
                let mut indicators = Vec::new();
                if ep.auth_required {
                    indicators.push("🔒");
                }
                if ep.deprecated {
                    indicators.push("⚠");
                }
                
                if !indicators.is_empty() {
                    summary = format!("{} {}", summary, indicators.join(" "));
                }
                
                table.add_row_cells([
                    method_styled,
                    ep.path.clone(),
                    summary,
                ]);
            }
            
            content.push(format!("{}", table.render_to_string()));
            content.push(String::new());
        }
        
        // Legend
        content.push(theme.style_text("  🔒 = Authentication required", "dim"));
        content.push(theme.style_text("  ⚠ = Deprecated", "dim"));
        content.push(String::new());
        
        let panel = Panel::from_text(&content.join("\n"))
            .title("API Endpoints")
            .rounded();
        
        output.renderable(&panel);
    }
}
```

### Integration with OpenAPI Crate
```rust
// In fastapi-openapi/src/lib.rs

impl OpenApiSpec {
    pub fn display_endpoints(&self) {
        let endpoints: Vec<EndpointInfo> = self.paths.iter()
            .flat_map(|(path, ops)| {
                ops.iter().map(|(method, op)| EndpointInfo {
                    method: method.clone(),
                    path: path.clone(),
                    operation_id: op.operation_id.clone(),
                    summary: op.summary.clone(),
                    tags: op.tags.clone(),
                    deprecated: op.deprecated,
                    auth_required: !op.security.is_empty(),
                    response_codes: op.responses.keys().cloned().collect(),
                })
            })
            .collect();
        
        OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
            endpoints,
            group_by_tag: true,
            show_responses: false,
        }.display();
    }
}
```

- [ ] Plain text fallback

impl HelpDisplay {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        
        // Banner (optional ASCII art)
        if let Some(banner) = self.render_banner(theme) {
            output.print(&banner);
        }
        
        // Usage line
        if let Some(usage) = &self.usage {
            output.print(&format!("Usage: {}", theme.style_text(usage, "bold")));
            output.print("");
        }
        
        // Sections
        for section in &self.sections {
            output.print(&theme.style_text(&format!("{}:", section.name), "bold"));
            
            let mut table = Table::new()
                .with_column(Column::new("").width(20))
                .with_column(Column::new("").max_width(50));
            
            for item in &section.items {
                let name_str = self.format_item_name(item, theme);
                let desc_str = self.format_item_description(item, theme);
                
                table.add_row_cells([name_str, desc_str]);
            }
            
            output.renderable(&table);
            output.print("");
        }
    }
    
    fn format_item_name(&self, item: &HelpItem, theme: &FastApiTheme) -> String {
        let mut parts = Vec::new();
        
        // Short alias first
        if let Some(short) = item.aliases.first() {
            parts.push(theme.style_text(short, "cyan"));
        }
        
        // Long name
        parts.push(theme.style_text(&format!("--{}", item.name), "cyan"));
        
        // Value hint
        if let Some(hint) = &item.value_hint {
            parts.push(theme.style_text(&format!(" <{}>", hint), "dim"));
        }
        
        parts.join(", ")
    }
    
    fn format_item_description(&self, item: &HelpItem, theme: &FastApiTheme) -> String {
        let mut desc = item.description.clone();
        
        if let Some(default) = &item.default {
            desc = format!("{} [default: {}]", desc, default);
        }
        
        if item.required {

### Overview
Create a beautiful validation error display that shows field-level errors, expected types, and nested validation failures in a clear, actionable format.

### Background & Reasoning
Validation errors are one of the most common issues developers face. Clear error messages:
- Pinpoint exactly which field failed
- Show what was expected vs what was received
- Handle nested structures clearly
- Group multiple errors logically

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structures
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/validation_error.rs

pub struct ValidationErrorDisplay {
    pub errors: Vec<ValidationError>,
    pub source_type: String,  // e.g., "Json<CreateUser>"
    pub context: Option<String>,
}

pub struct ValidationError {
    pub path: Vec<String>,     // ["user", "email"]
    pub message: String,
    pub code: String,          // e.g., "email", "required", "min_length"
    pub expected: Option<String>,
    pub received: Option<String>,
    pub nested: Vec<ValidationError>,
}

```rust
impl ValidationErrorDisplay {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        let mut content = Vec::new();
        
        // Header
        content.push(String::new());
        content.push(format!(
            "  Failed to validate: {}",
            theme.style_text(&self.source_type, "cyan")
        ));
        
        if self.errors.len() > 1 {
            content.push(format!("  {} validation errors", self.errors.len()));
        }
        
        content.push(String::new());
        
        // Errors
        for error in &self.errors {
            self.render_error(&mut content, error, 0, theme);
            content.push(String::new());
        }
        
        let panel = Panel::from_text(&content.join("\n"))
            .title("Validation Error")
            .rounded();
        
        output.renderable(&panel);
    }
    
    fn render_error(
        &self,
        content: &mut Vec<String>,
        error: &ValidationError,
        depth: usize,
        theme: &FastApiTheme,
    ) {
        let indent = "  ".repeat(depth + 1);
        let path = error.path.join(".");
        
        // Error path
        content.push(format!(
            "{}✗ {}",
            indent,
            theme.style_text(&path, "red bold")
        ));
        
        // Nested or leaf error
        if error.nested.is_empty() {
            // Leaf error
            let connector = if depth > 0 { "└──" } else { "│" };
            content.push(format!(
                "{}  {} {}",
                indent,
                connector,
                error.message
            ));
            
            if let Some(expected) = &error.expected {
                content.push(format!(
                    "{}      Expected: {}",
                    indent,
                    theme.style_text(expected, "green")
                ));
            }
            
            if let Some(received) = &error.received {
                content.push(format!(
                    "{}      Received: {}",
                    indent,
                    theme.style_text(received, "red")
                ));
            }
        } else {
            // Nested errors
            for (i, nested) in error.nested.iter().enumerate() {
                let is_last = i == error.nested.len() - 1;
                let prefix = if is_last { "└──" } else { "├──" };
                
                content.push(format!("{}  {} ", indent, prefix));
                self.render_error(content, nested, depth + 1, theme);
            }
        }
    }
    
    fn display_plain(&self, output: &RichOutput) {
        output.plain(&format!("Validation Error: {}", self.source_type));
        
        for error in &self.errors {
            self.render_error_plain(output, error, "");
        }
    }
    
    fn render_error_plain(&self, output: &RichOutput, error: &ValidationError, prefix: &str) {
        let path = error.path.join(".");
        
        if error.nested.is_empty() {
            let mut msg = format!("- {}{}: {}", prefix, path, error.message);
            
            if let (Some(exp), Some(rec)) = (&error.expected, &error.received) {
                msg.push_str(&format!(" (expected: {}, got: {})", exp, rec));
            }
            
            output.plain(&msg);
        } else {
            for nested in &error.nested {
                self.render_error_plain(output, nested, &format!("{}{}", prefix, path));
            }
        }
    }
}
```

impl<T: DeserializeOwned + Validate> FromRequest for Json<T> {
    type Error = HttpError;
    
    async fn from_request(req: &Request, cx: &Cx) -> Result<Self, Self::Error> {
        let value: T = serde_json::from_slice(req.body()?)
            .map_err(|e| /* parse error */)?;
        
        if let Err(errors) = value.validate() {
            let display = ValidationErrorDisplay {
                errors: errors.into(),
                source_type: format!("Json<{}>", std::any::type_name::<T>()),
                context: None,
            };
            display.display();  // Only displays if debug enabled
            
            return Err(HttpError::UnprocessableEntity(errors));
        }
        
        Ok(Json(value))
    }

Essential for file serving and SPA routing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:51:29.208480863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:38:52.795218911Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:38:52.795153479Z","close_reason":"Implemented {*path} wildcard syntax for catch-all routes. Added 13 comprehensive tests covering file serving, SPA routing, priority, conflicts, and syntax equivalence.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ihr","depends_on_id":"bd-fhmy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:04.030031370Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-3ihr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
Wildcard/catch-all routes like `/files/{*path}` match any remaining path segments. Essential for:
- Static file serving: `/static/{*filepath}` matches `/static/js/app.js`
- SPA routing: `/{*route}` catches all frontend routes for client-side router
- Proxy routes: `/api/{*rest}` forwards to backend

## Current Limitation
Router only supports single-segment parameters `{id}`. No way to match multiple segments.

## Solution Architecture

### Route Pattern Syntax
```
/files/{*path}     -> matches /files/a, /files/a/b, /files/a/b/c
/api/v1/{*rest}    -> captures everything after /api/v1/
```

### Trie Implementation
Wildcard nodes are special leaf nodes that match any remaining path:
```rust
enum TrieNode {
    Static { segment: String, children: Vec<TrieNode> },
    Param { name: String, children: Vec<TrieNode> },
    Wildcard { name: String, route: Route },  // Always a leaf
}
```

### Matching Priority
1. Static segments (exact match)
2. Single-segment parameters
3. Wildcard catch-all (lowest priority)

### Captured Value
Wildcard captures entire remaining path:
```rust
// Route: /files/{*path}
// Request: /files/css/styles/main.css
// Captured: path = "css/styles/main.css"
```

## Files to Modify
- crates/fastapi-router/src/lib.rs - Trie and Router
- crates/fastapi-router/src/trie.rs (if separate)

## Dependencies
- Should coordinate with bd-5bt0 (route priority rules)
- Should update bd-2b8n (type converters) - wildcard is always String

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `{*name}` syntax recognized in route patterns
- [ ] Wildcard matches zero or more segments
- [ ] Captured value is complete remaining path
- [ ] Wildcard has lowest priority (static > param > wildcard)
- [ ] Only one wildcard per route (at end)
- [ ] Tests cover edge cases

This blocks the entire rich output feature from compiling.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:48:58.676457053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:15:45.315586191Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:15:38.361563448Z","close_reason":"Fixed rich_rust dependency - using git dependency, re-enabled fastapi-output in workspace, fixed compile errors in routing_debug.rs","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jgt","depends_on_id":"bd-nww8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:40.385854229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"bd-3jgt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
The fastapi-output crate provides rich terminal output for development (colored logs, ASCII banners, styled errors). It depends on rich_rust for the terminal rendering capabilities.

## Current Problem
In crates/fastapi-output/Cargo.toml:
```toml
[dependencies.rich_rust]
path = "../../../rich_rust"  # This path doesn't exist!
optional = true
```

The path assumes rich_rust is a sibling of the fastapi_rust workspace root, but:
1. rich_rust may not be checked out at that location
2. This breaks `cargo build` for the entire workspace when the feature is enabled
3. CI/CD pipelines will fail

## Impact
- `cargo build -p fastapi-output --features rich` fails
- Cannot test or use rich output features
- Blocks Phase 2+ of rich output integration
- Makes fastapi-output effectively unusable

## Solution Options

### Option 1: Use crates.io (Recommended if published)
```toml
[dependencies]
rich_rust = { version = "0.1", optional = true }
```
Pros: Standard, portable, versioned
Cons: Requires rich_rust to be published

### Option 2: Git dependency
```toml
[dependencies.rich_rust]
git = "https://github.com/user/rich_rust"
optional = true
```
Pros: Works before crates.io publish
Cons: Slower builds, no offline

### Option 3: Workspace member
Move rich_rust into this workspace as a crate
Pros: Full control, workspace optimization
Cons: Bloats this repo if rich_rust is separate project

### Option 4: Conditional path with fallback
```toml
[target.'cfg(feature = "rich-local")'.dependencies]
rich_rust = { path = "../rich_rust" }
```
For development only

## Recommendation
1. Short-term: Option 2 (git dependency) to unblock development
2. Long-term: Option 1 (crates.io) when rich_rust is published

## Files to Modify
- crates/fastapi-output/Cargo.toml

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `cargo build -p fastapi-output --features rich` succeeds (if feature exists)
- [ ] CI passes
- [ ] Document the dependency strategy in README

## Dependencies

## Considerations
- rich_rust is Dan's custom crate - need to verify it's ready for external use
- May need to fork/vendor if not available
- Consider making rich optional with fallback to plain output

All 59 router tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kxd","depends_on_id":"bd-229o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:59:31.910506135Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kxd","depends_on_id":"bd-fhmy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:56:03.014972651Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-3kxd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background & Context
The Route struct in fastapi-router/src/lib.rs currently stores route metadata (method, pattern, converter, path) but is missing the most essential field - the handler function pointer that should be invoked when a route matches.

## Current Implementation Gap
```rust
pub struct Route {
    pub method: Method,
    pub pattern: String,
    pub converter: Option<&'static str>,
    pub path: String,
    // MISSING: handler field!
}
```

## Why This Is Critical
- Routes can be registered and matched, but there's no way to dispatch to handlers
- The Router.lookup() returns Option<&Route> but Route contains no handler
- This makes the entire framework non-functional for its primary purpose
- ALL other features depend on this working (extractors, middleware, responses)

## Solution Architecture
The handler field should store a type-erased async function. Options considered:

### Option 1: Box<dyn Fn> (Recommended)
```rust
type Handler = Box<dyn Fn(&Cx, Request) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = Response> + Send>> + Send + Sync>;
```
Pros: Simple, works with closures
Cons: Double indirection, heap allocation per call

### Option 2: Arc<dyn HandlerTrait>
```rust
trait Handler: Send + Sync {
    fn call(&self, cx: &Cx, req: Request) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = Response> + Send>>;
}
```
Pros: Allows shared handlers, extensible
Cons: More complexity

### Option 3: Generic Handler<H>
Pros: Zero-cost, monomorphized
Cons: Requires type erasure at registration anyway

## Recommendation
Use Option 1 with a Handler type alias. Simple, works, can optimize later.

## Files to Modify
1. crates/fastapi-router/src/lib.rs - Add handler field to Route, update Router
2. May need Handler trait in fastapi-core for reuse

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Route struct has handler field
- [ ] Router::add() accepts handler parameter
- [ ] Router::lookup() returns route with callable handler
- [ ] Handler works with async functions via Cx
- [ ] All existing router tests pass
- [ ] New tests verify handler dispatch

## Dependencies
None - this is foundational

## Estimated Complexity
Medium - straightforward addition but needs careful async handling

## Task Overview

Create the mode.rs module with the OutputMode enum and logic for selecting and switching
between output modes at runtime.

## The Three Modes

### Rich Mode
- Full rich_rust styling with colors, boxes, tables
- Used when: Human terminal detected, FORCE_COLOR set
- Requires: rich feature enabled, rich_rust available

### Plain Mode  
- Structured plain text, no ANSI codes
- Used when: Agent detected, NO_COLOR set, not TTY
- Fallback when: rich feature disabled
- Format: Predictable, parseable text (e.g., "[OK] Message", "[ERROR] Details")

### Minimal Mode
- Colors only, no box characters or complex formatting
- Used when: Want some visual polish but compatibility concerns
- Useful for: Older terminals, limited unicode support

## File: crates/fastapi-output/src/mode.rs

```rust
//\! Output mode selection and switching.
//\!
//\! This module defines the three output modes and provides logic
//\! for selecting the appropriate mode based on environment detection.

use crate::detection::{is_agent_environment, detected_preference, OutputPreference};

/// Output rendering mode.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
pub enum OutputMode {
    /// Full rich_rust styling: colors, boxes, tables, unicode.
    Rich,
    
    /// Plain text output with no ANSI codes or special characters.
    #[default]
    Plain,
    
    /// Colors only, no box characters or complex formatting.
    Minimal,
}

impl OutputMode {
    /// Select the appropriate mode based on environment detection.
    pub fn auto() -> Self {
        // Check for explicit preference via FASTAPI_OUTPUT_MODE
        if let Ok(mode_str) = std::env::var("FASTAPI_OUTPUT_MODE") {
            if let Ok(mode) = mode_str.parse::<OutputMode>() {
                eprintln\!("[DEBUG] OutputMode::auto() explicit override: {:?}", mode);
                // Rich requires feature flag
                if matches\!(mode, OutputMode::Rich) {
                    #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
                    return OutputMode::Rich;
                    #[cfg(not(feature = "rich"))]
                    {
                        eprintln\!("[DEBUG] Rich mode requested but feature disabled, falling back to Plain");
                        return OutputMode::Plain;
                    }
                }
                return mode;
            }
        }
        
        // Auto-detect based on environment
        let pref = detected_preference();
        eprintln\!("[DEBUG] OutputMode::auto() detected_preference: {:?}", pref);
        
        match pref {
            OutputPreference::Plain => OutputMode::Plain,
            OutputPreference::Rich => {
                #[cfg(feature = "rich")]
                return OutputMode::Rich;
                #[cfg(not(feature = "rich"))]
                return OutputMode::Plain;
            }
        }
    }
    
    /// Check if this mode uses ANSI color codes.
    pub fn uses_colors(&self) -> bool {
        matches\!(self, Self::Rich | Self::Minimal)
    }
    
    /// Check if this mode uses box-drawing characters.
    pub fn uses_boxes(&self) -> bool {
        matches\!(self, Self::Rich)
    }
    
    /// Check if this mode supports tables.
    pub fn supports_tables(&self) -> bool {
        matches\!(self, Self::Rich)
    }
    
    /// Get the status indicator for success in this mode.
    pub fn success_indicator(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::Rich => "✓",
            Self::Plain => "[OK]",
            Self::Minimal => "[OK]",
        }
    }
    
    /// Get the status indicator for errors in this mode.
    pub fn error_indicator(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::Rich => "✗",
            Self::Plain => "[ERROR]",
            Self::Minimal => "[ERROR]",
        }
    }
    
    /// Get the status indicator for warnings in this mode.
    pub fn warning_indicator(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::Rich => "⚠",
            Self::Plain => "[WARN]",
            Self::Minimal => "[WARN]",
        }
    }
    
    /// Get the status indicator for info in this mode.
    pub fn info_indicator(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::Rich => "ℹ",
            Self::Plain => "[INFO]",
            Self::Minimal => "[INFO]",
        }
    }
}

impl std::fmt::Display for OutputMode {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        match self {
            Self::Rich => write\!(f, "rich"),
            Self::Plain => write\!(f, "plain"),
            Self::Minimal => write\!(f, "minimal"),
        }
    }
}

impl std::str::FromStr for OutputMode {
    type Err = OutputModeParseError;
    
    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {
            "rich" => Ok(Self::Rich),
            "plain" => Ok(Self::Plain),
            "minimal" => Ok(Self::Minimal),
            _ => Err(OutputModeParseError(s.to_string())),
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct OutputModeParseError(String);

impl std::fmt::Display for OutputModeParseError {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        write\!(f, "invalid output mode '{}', expected: rich, plain, minimal", self.0)
    }
}

impl std::error::Error for OutputModeParseError {}
```

The framework needs end-to-end integration tests that verify the complete request lifecycle through all crates working together. Currently each crate has unit tests but no tests verify the full integration.

## Test Coverage Needed
1. **HTTP Parsing -> Router**: Raw bytes parsed, path extracted, route matched
2. **Router -> Extractors**: Route params, query string, headers extracted
3. **Extractors -> Handler**: Extracted values passed to handler function
4. **Handler -> Response**: Handler return value converted to Response
5. **Response -> HTTP**: Response serialized to bytes and sent

## Example Test Structure
```rust
#[test]
fn full_request_lifecycle() {
    let app = App::new()
        .route(get_user);
    
    let client = TestClient::new(app);
    
    // This should exercise: HTTP parse -> route match -> extract Path<i64> -> 
    // call handler -> serialize Json -> format HTTP response
    let response = client.get("/users/42").send();
    
    assert_eq!(response.status(), 200);
    assert_eq!(response.json::<User>().id, 42);
}
```

## Scenarios to Test
1. GET with path parameters
2. POST with JSON body
3. Query string extraction
4. Header extraction
5. Multiple extractors in one handler
6. Middleware chain execution
7. Error handler invocation
8. 404 for unknown routes
9. 405 for wrong method
10. Validation errors

## Files to Modify
- Create: crates/fastapi/tests/integration/
- Create: crates/fastapi/tests/integration/lifecycle.rs

## Dependencies
- Depends on bd-2pl3 (App.serve()) for full server testing
- Depends on bd-3kxd (Route handler) for handler dispatch
- Uses existing TestClient from fastapi-core

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests cover complete request lifecycle
- [ ] Tests run as part of CI
- [ ] Tests use TestClient (no actual network)
- [ ] Each major scenario has dedicated test
- [ ] Tests document expected behavior
